title: RFC: Digisign 4W - Spouse Signing

td.p: Antoni Santoso, RFC prepared by Codex
TODO: Vehicle Finance backend, Digisign, BFI integration, Product, QA
Enable backend support for 4W borrower Digisign with spouse signing requirements, while preserving existing 2W and non-spouse-required flows.
Car-backed vehicle financing product. In this service it maps to ProductType.CAR value 1.
Loan agreement document generated by BFI and signed through Digisign.
Additional signature flow required when BFI sends verified spouse information and spouse LBA data on approval.
Manual offline signing fallback when spouse KYC or spouse signing cannot proceed digitally.

p: Vehicle Finance currently uses Digisign for 2W borrower LBA signing. 4W borrowers have remained on manual signing because married borrowers can require spouse signing, and the existing digital signing chain only represents borrower and lender signing. The PRD asks backend to support 4W Digisign by accepting spouse data and spouse LBA information from BFI, exposing spouse signing state to clients, and ensuring the loan does not progress until the required signing parties have completed the process.
The target users are all 4W user groups. Product rollout is expected to start with merchant and driver user groups, then expand to consumer if no issues are observed.

ul.li: BFI sends final spouse data and spouse LBA information in the approved callback when spouse signing is required.
If BFI does not send spouse signing data, MAB treats spouse signing as not required.
Spouse data includes full name, phone number, e-KTP/NIK, date of birth, and place of birth. Variant B also mentions spouse KYC result from BFI.
The spouse must be whitelisted automatically for Vehicle Finance based on the phone number from BFI.
The spouse app needs an entrypoint to sign LBA, with FIFO behavior if one spouse is related to multiple borrowers.
The borrower app must show spouse signing status, expiry date, spouse name, and spouse phone number.
Borrower and spouse signing can happen in parallel. Lender signing happens after borrower and spouse signing.
If spouse KYC is rejected or blocked, the flow falls back to wet signing outside the app.

p: When BFI sends an approved loan callback, the loan service updates the loan status and then checks whether Digisign should be processed. If Digisign is enabled and the callback contains document data, the service starts an asynchronous LBA document generation process. The LBA service fetches the latest BFI loan detail, takes the first document from the first vehicle asset, converts it into the local LBA document shape, uploads the BFI LBA file to OSS, stores a loan_documents record, marks the loan metadata as Digisign-enabled, and sends the borrower Digisign notification.

After the borrower starts signing, DocumentSigningService creates or updates a digisign_processes record for BORROWER_SIGNING, uploads the stored LBA to Digisign, and submits the signing request with borrower phone, NIK, and GoPay account ID. When the borrower callback completes with a valid result, the service immediately creates a LENDER_SIGNING event. When the lender callback completes, the complete document signing event is emitted.

ul.li: The BFI document DTO only represents the current borrower/lender LBA shape. It does not model spouse data, spouse KYC result, or spouse signature coordinates.
The LBA service uses only the first BFI document from the first vehicle asset, so it cannot distinguish borrower LBA from spouse LBA if BFI sends multiple documents.
DigisignProcessType only contains BORROWER_SIGNING and LENDER_SIGNING, so there is no durable spouse signing state.
The borrower callback currently starts lender signing immediately. This violates the new PRD sequence when spouse signing is required.

Document signing resolves the borrower customer by loan ID and uses the borrower GoPay account ID for Digisign headers. There is no existing repository port for resolving the spouse GoPay account ID from spouse phone number.
There is no backend response model for spouse signing entrypoint/status, FIFO spouse tasks, or borrower-visible spouse status.

td.p: Approved loan update delegates to Digisign handling after updating status.
Document processing requires Digisign feature flag and non-empty BFI document URL.
LBA service saves one loan document and stores BFI LBA document metadata in document_info.
Document signing creates per-process digisign_processes records and uploads documents to Digisign.
Borrower callback immediately emits LENDER_SIGNING when borrower signing succeeds.
Signing upload uses the borrower customer resolved from loan ID and passes borrower GoPay account ID.

p: For 4W loans, the backend can process BFI approval callbacks that include spouse signing data. If spouse data is present, the backend persists spouse signing requirement details, creates spouse signing state, exposes spouse signing entrypoint/status APIs, and waits for both borrower and spouse signing before starting lender signing. If spouse data is absent, the existing borrower-to-lender flow remains unchanged.
callout: This RFC records a backend design proposal. Items marked TODO need confirmation before implementation starts.

td.p: Extend existing Digisign/LBA document backend APIs and add spouse-specific read endpoints only where no existing API can serve spouse entrypoint/status.
Repo evidence: existing loan document APIs and Digisign callbacks.
BFI callback is the source of truth for final spouse data and spouse signing requirement. Local loan document and digisign_processes records are source of truth for MAB signing state.
Borrower and spouse signing can happen in parallel. Lender signing starts only after all required borrower/spouse signing processes are SIGNED.
PRD question list confirms lender signing after borrower and spouse.
TODO: Confirm service or API for resolving spouse phone number to GoPay account ID and customer identity. This repo currently has customer lookup by customer ID and GoPay account ID, not by phone.
Variant B says BFI may provide spouse KYC result. TODO: Confirm whether MAB trusts BFI spouse KYC result or must trigger OneKYC before exposing LBA.
No cache by default. Status and entrypoint APIs read durable database state. TODO: Add cache only if status read volume requires it.
Use existing Digisign feature flag plus a new 4W spouse signing rollout guard by user group. Start merchant and driver, then consumer.
No backfill for existing manual-signing 4W loans unless Product explicitly requests migration. New callbacks populate spouse state going forward.
Reuse digisign_processes retry model for upload/submit/callback failures. Duplicate callbacks should be idempotent by process ID and process type.

ul.li: Backend parsing and persistence of BFI spouse data and spouse LBA metadata when present.
Spouse signing state in the existing Digisign process model.
Borrower and spouse status APIs needed by app surfaces.
Spouse entrypoint API for the spouse app, including FIFO ordering for multiple pending spouse signing tasks.
Notification event trigger for immediate spouse signing reminder when BFI spouse data is received.
Backend guards so lender signing and loan processing proceed only after required borrower and spouse signing are complete.
Frontend bottom sheet, floating action button, and UI copy implementation.
WhatsApp template content and delivery if the messaging platform is not owned by this backend.
Manual wet-sign operations outside MAB systems.
Changing BFI ownership of final verified spouse data.
Backfilling existing 4W manual signing loans unless separately approved.

p: Extend the existing loan document and Digisign flow instead of creating a new service. BFI remains the owner of final spouse verification data. On approved callback, the backend detects whether spouse signing data exists. If absent, it follows the current flow. If present, it stores spouse information and spouse LBA details in the existing loan document domain, creates or exposes a SPOUSE_SIGNING process, and makes spouse signing visible through backend APIs.

Borrower and spouse signing should be independently startable after LBA data is ready. The backend records each signing attempt in digisign_processes. Once a borrower callback or spouse callback is completed, the service checks whether all required human signers are SIGNED. Only then does it emit the existing LENDER_SIGNING event. The lender completion callback continues to emit the complete document signing event.

For spouse entrypoint lookup, the spouse app needs a stable identity. The PRD gives spouse phone number from BFI, while the existing Digisign client requires a GoPay account ID header. The implementation must add or consume an identity lookup that maps spouse phone to GoPay account ID before spouse signing can be submitted to Digisign. This remains an explicit TODO because no such port exists in the repository evidence.
h1: User Story: BFI Spouse Data And LBA Intake
p: As MAB, I want to receive final spouse data and spouse LBA data from BFI on approval, so that backend can start the correct 4W spouse signing flow.

td.p: BFI approval callback contains spouse data and spouse LBA data
Backend persists spouse signing requirement, spouse identity fields, spouse LBA metadata, and expiry time.
BFI approval callback does not contain spouse data
Backend treats spouse signing as not required and preserves the existing borrower-lender flow.
The same approval data is received again
Backend does not create duplicate loan document or duplicate signing process records.

p: Extend BFI loan document parsing to include spouse information, spouse KYC result if confirmed, and spouse signature coordinates. Update LBA conversion to process all relevant BFI documents rather than only the first document. Store borrower and spouse LBA metadata in the existing loan document domain, either as structured document_info fields or related child metadata. Prefer JSON-compatible evolution to avoid a disruptive table migration unless query needs require indexed columns.
whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant BFI participant BFIController participant LoanService participant LBADocumentService participant DB BFI->>BFIController: Approved callback with documents and optional spouse data BFIController->>LoanService: updateApprovedLoan LoanService->>LoanService: shouldProcessDigisignDocuments alt spouse data present LoanService->>LBADocumentService: initiate/get LBA LBADocumentService->>DB: persist borrower and spouse LBA metadata LBADocumentService->>DB: mark spouseSigningRequired=true else spouse data absent LoanService->>LBADocumentService: existing borrower LBA flow LBADocumentService->>DB: persist borrower LBA metadata only end
p: As a spouse, I want to see my pending LBA signing entrypoint, so that I can sign the LBA for my spouse's loan.

td.p: A spouse has at least one pending spouse signing task
Backend returns the oldest pending signing task first.
The spouse has no pending signing task
Backend returns no active spouse signing entrypoint.
Spouse KYC is rejected or blocked
Backend does not trigger digital signing and marks wet-sign fallback state for borrower visibility.

p: Add a backend read API for spouse signing entrypoint lookup by authenticated spouse identity. The lookup should use durable spouse signing records ordered by created time or approval callback time to satisfy FIFO. Response should include borrower masked name, expiry time, signing status, and a deeplink or signing initiation payload as confirmed by mobile and Digisign contracts. TODO: confirm whether spouse identity arrives as customer ID, GoPay account ID, phone, or a tokenized identity header.

td.p: vehicle-finance-api-provider / VF mobile app spouse surface
Authenticated GoPay/VF user. TODO: confirm exact headers and identity mapping.
401 for unauthenticated, 404 or empty active state for no task, 409 if task cannot proceed due to KYC/wet-sign state.
Client may retry reads. Backend should avoid external blocking calls on hot path where possible.
New endpoint. No change to existing borrower APIs unless clients opt in.

whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant SpouseApp participant VFAPI participant DB SpouseApp->>VFAPI: GET spouse signing entrypoint VFAPI->>VFAPI: resolve spouse identity VFAPI->>DB: find oldest pending spouse signing task alt pending task exists VFAPI-->>SpouseApp: entrypoint payload with expiry and status else no task VFAPI-->>SpouseApp: no active entrypoint end
h1: User Story: Borrower Tracks Spouse Signing
p: As a borrower, I want to track spouse signing status, so that I can follow up before the LBA expires.

td.p: Backend returns spouse name, phone number, expiry time, and status.
Backend returns no spouse signing requirement.
Borrower signed but spouse has not signed
Backend does not advance to lender signing or processing complete state.

p: Extend the existing loan document preview/detail response or add a companion status endpoint for borrower surfaces. The response should be driven by persisted spouse signing state, not by live polling Digisign. Expiry should come from BFI LBA metadata. Name/phone masking rules must be confirmed with Product and privacy.

td.p: TODO: Proposed extension to existing loan document preview or GET /loan-documents/{loanId}/signing-status
403 if borrower does not own loan, 404 if loan or document is absent, 200 with spouseSigningRequired=false when spouse data is absent.
Existing clients should ignore added nullable fields if the existing endpoint is extended.

whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant BorrowerApp participant VFAPI participant DB BorrowerApp->>VFAPI: GET signing status VFAPI->>DB: load loan document and digisign processes VFAPI->>VFAPI: compute borrower/spouse/lender readiness VFAPI-->>BorrowerApp: spouse status, expiry, spouse name and phone
p: As MAB, I want lender signing and loan progress to wait for required spouse signing, so that 4W compliance requirements are met.

td.p: Backend records borrower SIGNED and waits for spouse SIGNED before lender signing.
Backend records spouse SIGNED and waits for borrower SIGNED before lender signing.
Backend preserves the current borrower to lender chain.
A duplicate Digisign callback is received
Backend remains idempotent and does not emit duplicate lender signing events.

p: Add SPOUSE_SIGNING to the Digisign process model. Add a spouse callback endpoint or route existing callbacks by process type if Digisign can call a unified endpoint. Replace the direct borrower-callback-to-lender event with an all-required-human-signers-complete check. The check reads BORROWER_SIGNING and SPOUSE_SIGNING states for the loan document. If spouse signing is not required, the check returns true after borrower signing, preserving existing behavior.
whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant Digisign participant VFAPI participant DB participant Kafka Digisign->>VFAPI: Borrower or spouse callback COMPLETED VFAPI->>DB: update process status SIGNED VFAPI->>DB: load required signing process states alt borrower and required spouse signed VFAPI->>Kafka: emit LENDER_SIGNING else still waiting VFAPI-->>Digisign: callback accepted end

ul.li: Add or extend document_info JSON to store spouse signing requirement, spouse PII from BFI, spouse LBA metadata, spouse KYC result if confirmed, expiry time, and optional BFI document identifiers.
Add SPOUSE_SIGNING to DigisignProcessType. Existing digisign_processes table can store this enum value without schema changes if enum serialization is text-compatible.

TODO: Confirm whether spouse phone/NIK should be encrypted separately rather than stored only in JSON. This repo encrypts customer PII in customer converters, and spouse data should follow equivalent PII handling.

TODO: Add indexes only if spouse entrypoint lookup cannot be served efficiently from current tables. A normalized spouse_signing_tasks table may be preferable if querying by spouse account/phone is required at scale.

Immediate spouse reminder should be emitted when BFI spouse data is persisted. PN title: Tandatangan Dulu, Baru Cair! Subtitle: Yuk tandatangan, biar pinjaman pasangan kamu di GoPay Pinjam BPKB bisa cair.
Daily D+1 10:00 reminder requires scheduler ownership. TODO: Confirm whether this service or notification platform owns daily reminder scheduling.
WhatsApp content and CTA deeplink are still TBD in the PRD and must remain blocked until confirmed.

Add counters for spouse data received, spouse data absent, spouse signing required, spouse signing started, spouse signing completed, spouse signing failed, wet-sign fallback, and lender signing delayed by spouse.
Add callback metrics tagged by process type, status, result, and user group.
Dashboard funnel should support borrower, spouse, and lender signing durations and time difference between borrower and spouse signing.

ol.li: Ship parsing and persistence behind disabled 4W spouse signing feature flag.
Enable internal or allowlisted 4W callbacks in non-production, then production shadow logging without exposing spouse entrypoints.
Enable merchant and driver user groups for spouse entrypoint/status after callback data quality is verified.
Expand to consumer user group after no critical callback, signing, or KYC issues are observed.
Rollback by disabling the 4W spouse signing flag; callbacks should continue using existing borrower/non-spouse flow where safe, or route affected cases to wet sign.

ul.li: TODO: Confirm exact BFI callback JSON shape for spouse data, spouse LBA, spouse signature coordinates, spouse KYC result, and document role/type.
TODO: Confirm identity lookup from spouse phone to GoPay account ID, and behavior when phone is linked to a different NIK.
TODO: Confirm whether spouse can complete KYC in GoPay app during survey.
TODO: Confirm whether spouse LBA expiry is always the same as borrower LBA expiry.
TODO: Confirm whether MAB can send spouse KTP image/selfie to BFI if needed.
TODO: Confirm wet-sign state ownership and borrower UI status when spouse KYC is rejected.
Risk: storing spouse PII in document_info JSON could bypass established customer PII encryption patterns unless explicitly designed.
Risk: using the first BFI document will break if BFI sends borrower and spouse documents in a list without stable ordering.

checkbox: Backend: confirm BFI callback payload contract for spouse data, spouse LBA, document role, and spouse KYC result.
Backend: extend BFI callback DTOs and LBA conversion for spouse information and spouse signature metadata.
Backend: persist spouse signing requirement and spouse LBA metadata with PII-safe storage.
Backend: add SPOUSE_SIGNING process type and callback handling.
Backend: change borrower callback gate so lender signing starts only after all required human signers are SIGNED.
Backend: add spouse entrypoint API with FIFO pending task behavior.
Backend: add borrower signing status API or extend existing document preview response.
Backend: add spouse reminder notification trigger and metrics.
QA: cover no-spouse, spouse-required, borrower-first, spouse-first, duplicate callback, expired LBA, and wet-sign fallback paths.
Release: configure feature flag, rollout allowlist, dashboards, and rollback procedure.
